<thought>
I am at row 02 column B facing right (`>`). The goal `O` is at row 08 column H. The cell directly ahead at row 02 column C is safe (`.`), so the best next move is to advance toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
FRENTE
</action>

<thought>
I am now at row 02 column C facing right. Moving forward to row 02 column D is safe and continues toward the goal, so I should keep moving ahead.
</thought>

<thought>

</thought>
<action>
FRENTE
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 02 column F facing right. The cell directly ahead at row 02 column G is lava, so I cannot move forward. The safe route toward the goal is to go down through the open corridor, which requires turning clockwise first to face down.
</thought>
<action>
GIRA_HORARIO
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 02 column F facing down. The cell ahead at row 03 column F is safe, and moving down continues me through the open corridor toward the goal at row 08 column H. I should move forward.
</thought>

<thought>

<thought>

</thought>

FRENTE

<thought>
I am at row 06 column F facing down. The cell ahead at row 07 column F is lava, so moving forward would be fatal. The safe route toward the goal is to turn left or right; to head toward the open upper area and then across, I should rotate counter-clockwise to face right? Actually from down, counter-clockwise means right? No: from down, counter-clockwise faces right? Down -> right is counter-clockwise on this grid orientation. I will turn counter-clockwise to avoid lava and aim toward the corridor.
</thought>
<action>
GIRA_ANTI_HORARIO
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 06 column F facing right. The cell ahead at row 06 column G is lava, so I must not move forward. To avoid the lava and progress toward the open area, I should turn counter-clockwise to face up.
</thought>
<action>
GIRA_ANTI_HORARIO
</action>

GIRA_HORARIO
